SEARCH
Package com.dua3.utility.io

Interface ReadableObjectStore

All Superinterfaces:
AutoCloseable
All Known Subinterfaces:
ObjectStore
All Known Implementing Classes:
FileObjectStore
public interface ReadableObjectStore extends AutoCloseable
Represents a generic object storage interface for managing and interacting with objects stored at specified URIs. The interface provides methods to list objects, read and write data, manage folders, and perform other common storage operations.

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    Optional<ObjectStore.ObjectInfo>
    getInfo(URI path)
    Get info about the object pointed to by the provided URI.
    URI
    getRoot()
    Retrieve the root URI that serves as the base location for objects managed by this storage.
    Stream<ObjectStore.ObjectInfo>
    list(URI path)
    Lists the objects located at the specified path within the storage.
    InputStream
    openInputStream(URI path)
    Opens an input stream to read data from the specified URI.
    default Stream<ObjectStore.ObjectInfo>
    walk(URI start)
    Traverses a directory structure starting at the given path and returns a stream of ObjectInfo instances representing the files and directories encountered during the traversal.
    default Stream<ObjectStore.ObjectInfo>
    walk(URI start, int maxDepth)
    Traverses a structure starting from the given URI and explores its elements up to a specified depth.

    Methods inherited from interface java.lang.AutoCloseable

    close

  • Method Details

    • getRoot

      URI getRoot()
      Retrieve the root URI that serves as the base location for objects managed by this storage. The URI returned will always be an absolute URI.
      Returns:
      the root URI representing the base location of the storage

    • list

      Stream<ObjectStore.ObjectInfo> list(URI path) throws IOException
      Lists the objects located at the specified path within the storage. If the path corresponds to a folder, the method will provide a stream of ObjectStore.ObjectInfo objects representing the metadata of the folder's contents.

      Notes:

      • The caller must close the returned stream.
      • Implementations must ensure that the object graph is acyclic, or define how cycles are handled.
      Parameters:
      path - the URI of the folder or object to be listed; must not be absolute.
      Returns:
      a stream of ObjectStore.ObjectInfo instances representing the objects under the specified path
      Throws:
      AbsolutePathException - if the path is absolute
      IllegalPathException - if the path points outside the root of the storage
      ObjectNotFoundException - if the path does not point to an existing object
      NotAFolderException - if the path points to a non-folder object
      IOException - if an I/O error occurs while accessing the storage

    • openInputStream

      InputStream openInputStream(URI path) throws IOException
      Opens an input stream to read data from the specified URI. The method returns an InputStream for reading the contents of the object at the given path.
      Parameters:
      path - the URI of the object to be read
      Returns:
      an InputStream to read data from the specified URI
      Throws:
      AbsolutePathException - if the path is absolute
      IllegalPathException - if the path points outside the root of the storage
      ObjectNotFoundException - if the path does not point to an existing object
      IOException - if the object at the path is not a data object or an I/O error occurs while opening the input stream

    • getInfo

      Optional<ObjectStore.ObjectInfo> getInfo(URI path) throws IOException
      Get info about the object pointed to by the provided URI.
      Parameters:
      path - the URI to check
      Returns:
      An Optional containing an ObjectStore.ObjectInfo for the object at the URI, or an empty Optional if the URI does not point to an existing object.
      Throws:
      AbsolutePathException - if the path is absolute
      IllegalPathException - if the path points outside the root of the storage
      IOException - if an I/O error occurs while attempting to retrieve the object info.

    • walk

      default Stream<ObjectStore.ObjectInfo> walk(URI start) throws IOException
      Traverses a directory structure starting at the given path and returns a stream of ObjectInfo instances representing the files and directories encountered during the traversal.

      Note: The caller must close the returned stream!

      Parameters:
      start - the starting URI of the directory to traverse
      Returns:
      a stream of ObjectInfo objects corresponding to the entries in the directory structure
      Throws:
      AbsolutePathException - if the path is absolute
      IllegalPathException - if the path points outside the root of the storage
      ObjectNotFoundException - if the path does not point to an existing object
      IOException - if an I/O error occurs.

    • walk

      default Stream<ObjectStore.ObjectInfo> walk(URI start, int maxDepth) throws IOException
      Traverses a structure starting from the given URI and explores its elements up to a specified depth.

      Note: The caller must close the returned stream!

      Parameters:
      start - the URI representing the starting point of the structure to be traversed
      maxDepth - the maximum depth to which the traversal should occur
      Returns:
      a stream of ObjectStore.ObjectInfo instances representing the elements encountered during traversal
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if maxDepth is less than 0
      AbsolutePathException - if the path is absolute
      IllegalPathException - if the path points outside the root of the storage
      ObjectNotFoundException - if the starting point does not point to an existing object
      IOException - if an I/O error occurs during the traversal
